pi_items.source: Web search verified all three domains exist as authoritative regulatory sources: FDA has Digital Health Center of Excellence with SaMD guidance; Federal Register publishes medical device regulations; IMDRF is International Medical Device Regulators Forum with official SaMD documents

Web search confirmed: SaMD = Software as a Medical Device (IMDRF/SaMD WG/N10FINAL:2013); FDA = Federal Food and Drug Administration; IMDRF = International Medical Device Regulators Forum (official 2026 leadership in Singapore)

CareerOneStop and OneNet Online confirmed this is a recognized occupational classification. Regulatory Affairs Managers ensure compliance with laws/regulations in pharma, medical devices, biotech industries

privileged_info_summary: The task requires research from three real, authoritative regulatory sources: FDA (Federal Food and Drug Administration) operates the Digital Health Center of Excellence with official guidance on SaMD (Software as a Medical Device); Federal Register publishes medical device regulations and rules; IMDRF (International Medical Device Regulators Forum) publishes international SaMD guidance documents including N10 (Key Definitions, 2013) and N41 (Clinical Evaluation, 2017). The occupation 'Regulatory Affairs Managers' is a recognized professional role (O*NET 11-9199.01) responsible for ensuring regulatory compliance in medical device companies. All referenced regulatory domains and documentation are publicly available real sources, not synthetic data.
